subject: Joint Distributions
topic: §45 Joint Probability Distributions of Functions of Random Variables
question: Let $X$ and $Y$ be two random variables with joint density function $f_{XY}$. Compute the pdf of $U = Y - X$.
answer: $f_{Y-X}(a) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f_{XY}(y - a, y)\,dy$. If $X$ and $Y$ are independent then $f_{Y-X}(a) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f_X(y - a) f_Y(y)\,dy = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f_X(y) f_Y(a + y)\,dy$. 45.11 $f_U(u) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} |v| f_{XY}\left(v, \frac{u}{v}\right)\,dv$. If $X$ and $Y$ are independent then $f_U(u) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} |v| f_X(v) f_Y\left(\frac{u}{v}\right)\,dv$
explanation: Answer: $f_{Y-X}(a) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f_{XY}(y - a, y)\,dy$. If $X$ and $Y$ are independent then $f_{Y-X}(a) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f_X(y - a) f_Y(y)\,dy = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f_X(y) f_Y(a + y)\,dy$. 45.11 $f_U(u) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} |v| f_{XY}\left(v, \frac{u}{v}\right)\,dv$. If $X$ and $Y$ are independent then $f_U(u) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} |v| f_X(v) f_Y\left(\frac{u}{v}\right)\,dv$
